mundo.titulo: Mahmood announces new refugee sponsorship route into UK
mundo.resumen: The home secretary says "capped safe and legal" routes would help to restore confidence in the asylum system.

mundo.titulo: Hundreds of Heathrow and Gatwick flights delayed due to thunderstorms
mundo.resumen: More than 600 flights have been delayed at Heathrow and Gatwick, many due to stormy weather south of the UK.

mundo.titulo: Are we in for a summer of serial heatwaves?
mundo.resumen: Forecasters have suggested that summer is likely to be warmer than average with an increased chance of more heatwaves, as Simon King explains.

mundo.titulo: US strikes Iran after attack on cargo ship
mundo.resumen: Iran accuses the US of violating their deal and says it struck targets linked to American forces in response.

mundo.titulo: Meloni and Trump: A very public fall-out that is proving very hard to fix
mundo.resumen: Italy's PM was not long ago being called the "Trump whisperer", but their relationship has gone from public attacks to personal insults.

mundo.titulo: Is Andy Burnham Labour's saviour, or just its best bet?
mundo.resumen: Labour insiders give Laura Kuenssberg their take on the man tipped to replace Keir Starmer as PM.

negocios.titulo: Trump threatens 100% tariff on European nations over tech tax
negocios.resumen: The US president says "Numerous European countries" have been discussing bringing in such a levy.

negocios.titulo: Three unusual things about the King's tax bill
negocios.resumen: King Charles paid £12.9m in tax for 2024-2025 - here's what we know about his unique tax situation.

negocios.titulo: I'm back at home again after uni - here's how I'm making it work
negocios.resumen: Rising costs are forcing more young adults to live with their parents, here's how not to come to blows.

negocios.titulo: Power banks and vapes now biggest fire risk on planes
negocios.resumen: Lithium battery fires are the number one safety risk to aircraft, yet the number of devices found in hold bags has nearly doubled in a year.

negocios.titulo: Asia stock markets slide as tech shares slump
negocios.resumen: Trading on South Korea's Kospi index was halted for the third time this week to prevent panic selling.

negocios.titulo: What's happening to petrol prices now oil is back to pre-Iran war levels?
negocios.resumen: When the conflict began on 28 February, fuel costs jumped as the war disrupted the production and transportation of energy across the Middle East.

tecnologia.titulo: Asian AI startups launch Mythos-like  models as Anthropic’s export ban drags on
tecnologia.resumen: New models are launching in Asia that promise Mythos-like capabilities without fear of an export ban. U.S. AI labs may never recover this enormous market.

tecnologia.titulo: Trump Admin releases Anthropic  Mythos to be used by more than 100 US companies, agencies
tecnologia.resumen: Over 100 companies and government agencies are reportedly authorized to use Mythos 5, including their non-American employees.

tecnologia.titulo: FTC gives Musk the OK to acquire SpaceX alumni startup Mesh
tecnologia.resumen: Mesh came out of stealth in February with a $50 million Series A.

tecnologia.titulo: Corgi, the buzzy Y Combinator-backed insurance tech startup, says it didn’t steal an open source product
tecnologia.resumen: Corgi became embroiled in  controversy when Papermark accused it of stealing its software. Corgi says it did not, raising new questions about vibe coding.

tecnologia.titulo: Novak Djokovic has a new job —  advisor to private equity firm General Atlantic
tecnologia.resumen: General Atlantic has tapped tennis legend Novak Djokovic to serve as a global strategic advisor.

tecnologia.titulo: OpenAI limits GPT-5.6 rollout after government request, says restrictions shouldn’t be the norm
tecnologia.resumen: “We don’t believe this kind of government access process should become the long-term default,” says OpenAI. “It keeps the best tools from users, developers, enterprises, cyber defenders, and global partners who need them.”

tecnologia.titulo: This puzzle game’s simple premise hides surprising depth
tecnologia.resumen: What's the Password? has a simple concept: To solve each of the game's more than 100 puzzles, you have to type in the right four-digit password on a number pad. That might sound like a limited constraint. But the simplicity gives solo developer Dan DiIorio, better known as TrampolineTales, lots of room to play with […]
